seo.title: Building Customer Support with Amazon Bedrock Agents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with Amazon Bedrock Agents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Amazon Bedrock Agents enable powerful customer support bots with RAG knowledge bases, CRM tool integrations, and action groups for ticket creation and order lookups. However, issues like malformed Lambda responses, poor chunking in retrieval, guardrail failures, and session state loss can lead to inaccurate or non-compliant interactions. Vex provides post-processing verification on invoke_agent() responses to ensure response accuracy, PII filtering, tone consistency, policy compliance, and proper escalation triggers.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with Amazon Bedrock Agents
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on a Bedrock Agent configured with a foundation model like Anthropic Claude, connected to a knowledge base for FAQ and product docs retrieval via Amazon OpenSearch or Pinecone. Action groups use Lambda functions to integrate with CRM systems like Salesforce for order lookups and ticket creation. Guardrails enforce PII redaction and policy checks, while multi-turn sessions maintain context for complex support queries.

User queries trigger agent.invoke_agent(), which orchestrates retrieval-augmented generation and tool calls. Responses flow back through guardrails before reaching the user. Common integrations include API Gateway for frontend exposure and Step Functions for orchestration.

Vex wraps the final agent response with verify() to catch failures in Lambda formatting, retrieval relevance, guardrail gaps, and session continuity before delivery.

risks.name: Action group Lambda functions returning malformed responses
risks.description: Lambda functions in action groups return JSON not matching agent schema, causing invocation failures or hallucinated tool results
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies response structure and content validity post-invoke_agent()

risks.name: Knowledge base retrieval missing relevant documents due to chunking
risks.description: Poor chunking strategies fail to retrieve contextually relevant docs, leading to inaccurate RAG responses
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-checks response relevance against query and known support patterns

risks.name: Agent guardrails not catching domain-specific policy violations
risks.description: Built-in guardrails miss customer support policies like escalation triggers or branded tone requirements
risks.mitigation: Vex applies custom policy/compliance checks beyond Bedrock guardrails

risks.name: Session state loss between multi-turn agent invocations
risks.description: Context loss in multi-turn conversations leads to repetitive or inconsistent support interactions
risks.mitigation: Vex validates session continuity and conversation coherence

implementation.title: Basic Bedrock Agent Invocation (Python)
implementation.description: Core invoke_agent() call with session management for customer support workflow

implementation.title: CRM Action Group Lambda (Python)
implementation.description: Lambda function for order lookup and ticket creation in customer support action group
implementation.code:
import json
import boto3

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    # Parse agent action request
    action_group = event['actionGroup']
    api_path = event['apiPath']
    parameters = event['parameters']
    
    if api_path == '/lookupOrder':
        order_id = next(p['value'] for p in parameters if p['name'] == 'orderId')
        # Mock CRM lookup
        order_data = {'status': 'shipped', 'items': ['Widget A']}
        return {
            'actionGroup': action_group,
            'apiPath': api_path,
            'httpStatusCode': 200,
            'responseBody': json.dumps(order_data)
        }
    
    raise ValueError('Invalid API path')
